topic: A person learns their childhood bully became a therapist
raw_output:
[story 1]

The invitation felt heavy in my hand, the embossed card catching the afternoon light just right. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic, uneven rhythm. I smoothed the paper on the mahogany table, tracing the elegant script of the invitation. When I finally looked up, the familiar, slightly sneering face of Marcus was there, framed by soft lighting, wearing a tailored suit that somehow made him look entirely different. "You're coming, then?" he asked, a slight upturn at the corner of his mouth that used to send shivers down my spine. I swallowed, my throat suddenly dry. "Yes," I managed, my voice surprisingly steady despite the tremor in my hands. I couldn't stop the wide, almost breathless smile that stretched across my face. This was… unexpected.

[story 2]

I kept glancing at the clock on the wall, tapping my foot rapidly against the polished floorboards. Every minute stretched into an eternity, yet I couldn't seem to pull my gaze away from the door. When Marcus finally walked in, there was a noticeable shift in his demeanor; the hard edges that used to define him seemed softened, replaced by an almost gentle attentiveness. He offered a small, genuine smile, and I felt a warmth bloom in my chest, spreading outwards like a slow-motion wave. "Glad you could make it," he said, his tone remarkably calm. I practically bounced in my seat, unable to contain the sheer buoyancy bubbling up inside me. I leaned forward, eager to start the conversation, my usual shyness completely overridden by a potent sense of anticipation.

[story 3]

The air in the waiting room crackled with unspoken energy. I fiddled incessantly with the cuff of my sweater, my fingers twitching with suppressed movement. When Marcus finally appeared, he looked… composed. The usual sharpness in his eyes was tempered with a thoughtful depth I’d never witnessed before. A bright, almost dazzling smile lit up his face as he greeted me. "It's good to see you," he murmured, his voice lower than I remembered. A sudden lightness washed over me, making my shoulders relax instantly. I stood up, taking a deep, deliberate breath, and walked towards him, a feeling of pure, unadulterated eagerness propelling my steps forward. I couldn't wait to see what this new version of him would say.
